content.full_text:
March 19, 1992

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, #800
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest

Dear Sirs:

I am writing to ask your opinion concerning a potential conflict of interest of Larry Rotelli, Supervisor of the First District in Tuolumne County. Supervisor Rotelli works for Cal Sierra Disposal, a local refuse collection service, as a collector. Cal Sierra takes the refuse collected to the landfill, deposits it there, and pays all appropriate fees to both the landfill operator and the County. No revenue is received from the landfill operation going back to the refuse collector.

The County is currently in the process of certifying an EIR for a potential new landfill site. The relationship between the refuse collector, the County, and a landfill operator is expected to stay the same once the new landfill is opened.

The question is, does Mr. Rotelli have a conflict of interest in voting on the landfill or any other issues related to the development of the landfill, meaning a zoning change or voting to begin construction of the project?

We would appreciate your response as soon as possible due to the fact that this is an ongoing process.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

MARK A. MITTON
County Administrative Officer

MAM:ele
cc: Supervisor Rotelli
